Name: <:Event:1393361751260528691>Advent of the War Sun
Rules Text: At the end of setup, all players other than the Embers of Muaat player gain the War Sun unit upgrade technology.|The Embers of Muaat player purges their faction-specific promissory note, unlocks their commander, and places 1 additional War Sun in their home system.
Flavour Text: The looming monstrosity looked more like an island than it did a weapon. Still, if the reports were to be believed, this...thing...was capable of incredible destruction on a scale possibly surpassing that of the Gashlai's own weapon. Magmus flared up in frustration. How had the Humans managed to build something so massive undetected? Someone was to blame for this, and he meant to find out who.

Name: <:Event:1393361751260528691>Age of Commerce
Rules Text: Players do not have to be neighbors to perform transactions with each other.|Players do not have a maximum number of commodities; when they refresh commodities, they gain a number of commodities equal to their commodity value instead.|Players can share non-faction technology with other players as part of a transaction. When sharing technology, the receiving player gains that technology from their own deck; the sharing player does not lose the technology.
Flavour Text: Ashalla and Carth arrived at Tsion Station to no great fanfare. No welcome party awaited their arrival, no feast held in their honor. But the coffers of the Emirates opened every locked door and greased every palm. The network would grow. And grow. And grow.

Name: <:Event:1393361751260528691>Age of Exploration
Rules Text: Relics require only 2 matching fragments be purged instead of 3.|The Naaz-Rokha Alliance's _Fabrication_ faction ability and _Black Market Forgery_ promissory note do not require purged fragments to match.|All players can perform the following action:|**Action:** Exhaust Dark Energy Tap and choose a non-home edge system that contains your ships to roll 1 die. On a result of 1-4, draw a random unused red tile, on a result of 5-10, draw a random unused blue tile. Place that tile adjacent to the chosen system so that it is touching at least 2 non-home systems. Place a frontier token in the system if it does not contain any planets.
Flavour Text: "Almost got it. Just a little higher..."

Name: <:Event:1393361751260528691>Age of Fighters
Rules Text: During setup, all players gain the Fighter II unit upgrade technology; the Naalu Collective player gains Hybrid Crystal Fighter II technology instead.|Fighters that count against your fleet pool have _Capacity_ value of 1; fighters cannot transport other fighters.|Non-fighter ships are purged when they are destroyed.
Flavour Text: The two fighters shot past the flagging cruiser, reflective hulls blazing a crisp blue, a mirror of the icy halo that served as the duel's stage. Neck and neck, dipping and diving amongst the rings. They were enemies. Lovers. Killers. Creators. Their connection was beyond definition. It transcended the grit and desperation of war and attained something divine.

Name: <:Event:1393361751260528691>Call of the Void
Rules Text: After you move 1 or more units into the active system, if that system is in The Fracture, gain 1 command token.|When you activate a system in The Fracture, apply +1 the the move values of each of your ships.
Flavour Text: This place...it is a graveyard. I can't see it, but I can feel it. Souls all around me, yelling, screaming at me to leave. But I can't leave. Not yet. Maybe not ever. There is something here, I know it. I can find what happened here, I can.\n- Last log of Kel Zin, explorer

Name: <:Event:1393361751260528691>Civilized Society
Rules Text: During setup, turn all public objectives faceup.|There is no limit to the number of public objectives a player may score during the status phase.|The game does not immediately end when a player reaches the required number of victory points; instead, it ends at the end of that round's status phase, and the player with the most victory points wins. In the case of a tie, the tied players total the influence values of their controlled planets and unspent trade goods; the player or players with the highest total win the game.
Flavour Text: Connor stiffened. For once in his life, he felt ill at ease. This was all too fancy. He coughed, straightened his bowtie, and tugged at his collar. Why was it so damn hard to breathe? How the hell had he let Suffi talk him into this…

Name: <:Event:1393361751260528691>Conventions of War Abandoned
Rules Text: Each hit produced by _Bombardment_ rolls destroys 3 units instead of 1.|Players that have the _X-89 Bacterial Weapon_ technology can perform the following action:|**Action:** Exhaust _X-89 Bacterial Weapon_ to choose 1 planet in a system that contains 1 of your units that has _Bombardment_; purge its planet card and all attachments or legendary planet ability cards associated with it.|You are eliminated if all of your home system's planet cards are purged.
Flavour Text: Stillness. Not rage, not violence. Complete and utter silence. Stillness is the sound of slaughter. A horror that the galaxy has wrought with its own hands. With hellfire and flashing steel there can still be hope, but with stillness - with stillness there is nothing.

Name: <:Event:1393361751260528691>Cosmic Phenomenae
Rules Text: Anomalies are adjusted as follows:|**Nebulae:** The defender applies +3 to each of their ship's combat rolls in the nebula instead.|**Asteroid Fields:** Fighters without a move value do not participate in space combat in asteroid fields.|**Supernovas:** Units with _Production_ in or adjacent to supernovas have their _Production_ values increased by 1.|**Gravity Rifts:** You may apply an additional +1 to the _Move_ values of any of your ships moving out of gravity rifts; if you do, those ships are removed on a roll of 5 or lower.|**Entropic Scar:** Systems that contain entropic scars are adjacent to each other.
Flavour Text: Acamar unfolded their wings, bathing them in the light of the dying star. Perfection. Bliss.

Name: <:Event:1393361751260528691>Cultural Exchange Program
Rules Text: At the end of setup, shuffle the reference cards that correspond to each faction in play. Each player draws 1 of those card and takes all leaders that correspond to that faction. They belong to that player for the remainder of the game. Then, each player unlocks their commander.|The Obsidian/Firmament player does not participate in the Cultural Exchange Program. Instead, they begin the game with "The Obsidian" relic.
Flavour Text: Its origins rooted in the Salient Sun, Last Bastion represented a fusion of both Human and Xxcha sensibilities.

Name: <:Event:1393361751260528691>Dangerous Wilds
Rules Text: During setup, place neutral infantry on each hazardous planet equal to that planet's resource value.|At the end of the round, for each hazardous planet that is not controlled, replenish any neutral infantry that were destroyed during the game round.|When a player gains control of a hazardous planet from the planet deck, they may research 1 technology; they may ignore a number of that technology's prerequisites equal to the planet's resource value.
Flavour Text: A thundering crash signaled the arrival of the behemoth - two in fact - and the huntress gripped her rifle tight. No room for error now. She hoped the others had heard the commotion and were headed her way, but deep down she knew. She was on her own.

Name: <:Event:1393361751260528691>Hidden Agenda
Rules Text: During the agenda phase, only the speaker can talk; all other players must remain silent except when declaring action cards. Transactions cannot be performed during this phase.|When voting, players secretly and simultaneously write their desired outcome and number of votes and pass them to the speaker. After all players have voted, the speaker secretly tallies the results and reveals only the totals to the other players; the speaker reveals who voted for which outcome only when required to resolve the outcome.|The Argent Flight's votes are public and are known before the other players vote.
Flavour Text: The speaker motioned dismissively as she spoke "...and so forth." Absolute silence filled the hall as the stunned councilors tried to process what had just happened.

Name: <:Event:1393361751260528691>Mercenaries for Hire
Rules Text: At the start of the game, shuffle the alliance cards that correspond to the factions not in play and place them in the common play area with the card fronts faceup; this is the mercenary deck.|All players can perform the following action:|**Action:** Spend 3 trade goods to gain the top card of the mercenary deck and place it in your play area.|Players can use the abilities of the mercenaries in their play area.
Flavour Text: Suffi flicked through the unclaimed bounties. Boring. Pass. Too much trouble. Too low bounty. She paused to study one whose mugshot had been replaced by a caricature performing a rude gesture. Grinning, she accepted it. This one could be fun. Fun - or new friends!

Name: <:Event:1393361751260528691>Minor Factions
Rules Text: During setup, players are dealt 1 fewer blue tile. Before creating the galaxy, shuffle the reference cards for factions not being played and deal 1 to each player. In speaker order, each player places that faction's home system in the second ring, equidistant from players' home systems (see page 7 in the rulebook). Then, that player places 3 neutral infantry on that system's planets, split as evenly as possible. These systems are minor faction systems and do not count as home systems.|When a player controls each planet in minor faction system, they take that faction's alliance card from the deck or from the player that owned it previously.|Planets in minor faction systems gain all three planet traits (cultural, industrial, and hazardous).

Name: <:Event:1393361751260528691>Monuments to the Ages
Rules Text: When you would place a structure on a non-home planet, you may spend 5 trade goods to place a neutral space dock instead; this is a monument, and is not considered to be a unit of any type. There can only be 3 monuments in play at once.|At the start of the status phase, place 1 commodity beneath each monument; each monument is worth 1 victory point to the player that controls its planet for every 3 commodities beneath it.|When a player gains control of a planet that contains a monument, they may destroy that monument.
Flavour Text: The stone visage floated through space, a small part of a greater whole that no longer existed, the head having been cleanly severed from the body - whether through an act of war, vandalism, or nature, Tai was unsure.

Name: <:Event:1393361751260528691>Rapid Mobilization
Rules Text: After setup, put The Fracture into play. Then, each player simulataneously resolves the following effects in order:|Place 1 infantry onto each planet adjacent to your home system (or the Creuss Gate/The Sorrow, if playing those factions); gain control of and ready those planets but do not explore them.|Place 1 space dock on any planet you control and place your flagship and 3 fighters in its system.|Gain your breakthrough.|Research 1 technology; if you are the Nomad player, research 1 additional technology.
Flavour Text: The vast Mindnet fleet had appeared out of nowhere. Rin felt her vessel shudder and groan with each salvo. She calculated that the shielding would hold - for now.

Name: <:Event:1393361751260528691>Stellar Atomics
Rules Text: During setup, each player places one of their control tokens on this card.|All players can perform the following action:|**Action:** Discard your control token from this card to destroy all ground forces and structures on any non-home planet.|If you do not have a control token on this card, you cannot vote or play action cards during the agenda phase.
Flavour Text: Light blossomed on the planet's surface, a silent, almost peaceful procession of red-orange glow across the planet's northern hemisphere. Viktor grimaced. This was extreme, even for the Baron.

Name: <:Event:1393361751260528691>Total War
Rules Text: When a player destroys or produces hits that destroy another player's units, they place commodities from the supply equal to the combined cost of the units they destroyed on a planet they control in their home system (infantry and fighters are worth 1 each).|When a player gains control of a home planet that has commodities, they move those commodities to a planet they control in their own home system.|All players can perform the following action:|**Action:** Discard 10 commodities from planets in your home system to gain 1 victory point.
Flavour Text: As a child she had visited a museum back on Jord and seen a physical newspaper preserved behind glass, "WAR" printed in oppresive black ink across its header. Claire shuddered. Dark times lay ahead.

Name: <:Event:1393361751260528691>Weird Wormholes
Rules Text: After a ship moves using at least 1 alpha, beta, or gamma wormhole, roll 1 die and consult the following list:|Fighter > Destroyer > Cruiser > Dreadnought > Carrier > Flagship > War Sun (if researched)|For each result of **1-5**, replace that ship with the ship **before** it from your reinforcements, if able.|For each result of **6-10**, replace that ship with the ship **after** it from your reinforcements, if able.|If there are not ships of that type in your reinforcements, skip that type and replace it with the next available type.
Flavour Text: The energies surged around the Xxcha vessel, converging, intertwining. 00029A-V and 22410B-V... highly volatile. This would not end well.

Name: <:Event:1393361751260528691>Wild, Wild Galaxy
Rules Text: Action cards are adjusted as follows:|**Direct Hit**: Can be used against mechs|**Flank Speed**: Applies +2 _Move_ instead of +1|**Maneuvering Jets**: Cancels all _Space Cannon_ hits|**Morale Boost**: Applies +2 to die rolls instead of +1|**Sabotage**: Also take the canceled action card|**Shields Holding**: Can be used in ground combat|**Skilled Retreat**: Does not place a command token|**War Machine**: Reduces cost by 5 instead of 1|**Diplomatic Pressure**: Player must give 3 notes|Additionally, **Stellar Converter** and **Nova Seed** can be used against any planets and systems.
Flavour Text: Connor stared in near-disbelief at the Ral Nel ranchers as they drove the colossal er-beetles across the desert and back toward their nesting grounds.

Name: <:Event:1393361751260528691>Zealous Orthodoxy
Rules Text: The first player to score 2 secret objectives gains 1 victory point.|Then, place that faction’s alliance card on this card; all players gain that ability. That faction’s alliance promissory note is then purged.
Flavour Text: Inexplicably, the Brotherhood began to draw converts from amongst the other factions - even from the Flight - and it was not altogether uncommon to see Shikrai walking beneath the marbled arches of Darien.
